title: Indonesian Sambal Telur (Sambal Fried Egg)

subs.name: 400 g firm tofu blocks, fried until golden the same way

ingredients.name: dried red chillies, soaked in warm water 10 minutes

subs.name: 1 tbsp Kashmiri red chilli powder mixed with 1 tsp cayenne
1 tbsp lemon juice plus pinch of amchur

ingredients.name: lemongrass stalk, bruised and tied in a knot

steps.text: Heat 3 tbsp oil in a kadai on medium-high. Score the peeled hard-boiled eggs lightly with a knife and fry, turning, for 3–4 minutes until the surface blisters and turns deep golden. Remove and set aside.
Drain the soaked dried chillies and blend them with the garlic, shallots and tomato into a smooth red paste. Add a splash of water if needed to keep the blender moving.

Add 1 tbsp oil to the same kadai on medium heat. Add the lemongrass, then pour in the chilli paste. Fry, stirring frequently, for 6–8 minutes until the sambal darkens in colour, smells deeply fragrant and the oil begins to pool at the edges.
Add tamarind pulp, jaggery and salt. Stir for 1 minute, then add 50 ml water. Simmer for 2–3 minutes until a thick, clingy sauce forms.

Nestle the fried eggs into the sambal. Spoon the sauce over them and simmer gently for 2 more minutes so the eggs absorb the flavour. Remove the lemongrass stalk and serve immediately with steamed rice.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: egg → 400 g firm tofu blocks, fried until golden the same way (makes a vegan sambal tahu); dried-red-chilli → 1 tbsp Kashmiri red chilli powder mixed with 1 tsp cayenne (adjust cayenne for heat); tamarind → 1 tbsp lemon juice plus pinch of amchur (use at the same stage)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: A kadai is ideal for frying the eggs and building the sambal in the same vessel. Dried red chillies (byadgi or guntur) from any Indian masala shop give excellent colour and heat. Tamarind pulp (imli) and jaggery (gur) are pantry staples in most Indian kitchens. Lemongrass is increasingly stocked in city supermarkets; skip it if unavailable — the sambal still delivers.
